title: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies shifts focus to space
summary: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies is shifting its focus from rooftop applications to space, aiming to power orbital data centers and large satellite constellations with its thin-film solar panels.

title: Isar Aerospace to launch German-built Planet imaging satellite
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a contract with Planet’s German subsidiary to launch an imaging satellite, showcasing Germany's end-to-end space capabilities. This marks a significant milestone for both companies in the European space sector.

title: Polish space tech company Sybilla Technologies secures funds to enter U.S. market
summary: Sybilla Technologies, a Polish space tech company, secured a $10 million investment from BGK and 3TS Capital Partners to expand into the U.S. market. The funding aims to support the company's growth and international expansion efforts.

title: Making satellite communications more resilient
summary: Kymeta's Ryan Stevenson discusses the benefits and challenges of multi-orbit and multi-band connectivity in a single terminal for satellite communications. The conversation highlights efforts to make satellite communications more resilient.

title: Final Atlas 5 Amazon Leo mission launches
summary: The final Atlas 5 rocket launched on July 2, carrying a payload of Amazon's Leo satellites. This mission marked the last time the Atlas 5 vehicle will carry a satellite payload.

title: Report links Starliner problems to overconfidence and unrealistic schedules
summary: A report attributes Boeing's CST-100 Starliner technical issues to overconfidence, unrealistic schedules, and NASA's limited oversight. The findings highlight systemic challenges in the spacecraft's development.

title: FCC to vote on satellite licensing overhaul July 22
summary: The FCC will vote on July 22 to overhaul its satellite licensing process, aiming to streamline applications amid growing constellation plans. The changes seek to create a more efficient 'licensing assembly line' for large and complex satellite projects.

title: Unseen threats overhead: Drones endanger U.S. rocket launch sites
summary: Drones pose a significant threat to U.S. rocket launch sites, potentially causing millions in delays and affecting multiple launch providers. The issue highlights the need for better airspace management to prevent disruptions.

title: Vantor offers up-to-date imagery with WorldView 3D
summary: Vantor, formerly Maxar Intelligence, introduced WorldView 3D on July 1 to provide high-definition and up-to-date imagery to customers. The new offering aims to enhance data currency for various applications.

title: EchoStar’s satellite TV and wireless subsidiaries file for bankruptcy
summary: EchoStar's satellite TV and wireless subsidiaries have filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection as part of a prepackaged restructuring plan. The move follows the sale of spectrum to SpaceX and AT&T.

title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches 24 Starlink satellites from California
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launched 24 Starlink satellites from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California. The mission took place on Wednesday, July 1, 2026.

title: SpaceX fires up all 6 of Starship's engines ahead of 13th test flight (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully test-fired all six engines of its Starship vehicle for a full minute at its Starbase facility, marking a milestone ahead of its 13th test flight.

title: 'Silo' season 3: Release date & how to watch Apple TV's sensational post-apocalyptic saga
summary: Apple TV+ has announced the release date for 'Silo' season 3, continuing the post-apocalyptic saga. Fans can watch the series on the streaming platform.

title: Apollo 11 landing site, a cosmic Eagle and a blue-white star: 4 night sky targets to celebrate America 250
summary: The article highlights four celestial targets in the night sky that celebrate America's 250th anniversary, including the Apollo 11 landing site and the North America Nebula.

title: 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has observed an exoplanet orbiting a dead star, offering insights into the potential fate of our solar system. This discovery provides a glimpse into the distant future when the Sun becomes a white dwarf.

title: 'Flying saucer' arrives at NASA for Artemis 3 moon mission in time for World UFO Day | Space photo of the day for July 2, 2026
summary: A 'flying saucer' spacecraft has arrived at NASA in preparation for the Artemis 3 moon mission, coinciding with World UFO Day. The delivery marks a significant step toward the planned lunar landing.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Breaks Agency Streaming Record
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission achieved record-breaking viewership with over 149.4 million views across its live coverage, including launch, lunar flyby, and splashdown. The mission demonstrated strong global public engagement through 24/7 NASA-owned streaming platforms.

title: Good Morning, Earth!
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured a photo of an orbital sunrise from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. The ISS completes 16 orbits around Earth daily, experiencing 16 sunrises and sunsets.

title: NASA’s Webb Reveals Stars Sparking to Life in Cosmic Celebration
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured infrared images of stars forming in the FS Tau star system, revealing previously unseen details through thick cosmic dust. The image also showcases numerous background galaxies, adding to the celestial spectacle.

title: What’s Up: July 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for July 2026, highlighting a Moon-and-planets meetup, a comet, the Milky Way, and Saturn's rings at a new angle.

title: NASA’s Chandra Examines Milky Way at Arms’ Length
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory reveals that the Milky Way's outer spiral arms may extend farther than previously believed. This discovery could prompt astronomers to revise their understanding of the galaxy's structure.

title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for New Yearlong Simulated Moon, Mars Mission
summary: NASA is recruiting volunteers for a yearlong simulated Moon and Mars mission starting in August 2027. Participants will live in isolated conditions at Johnson Space Center to study interplanetary mission challenges.

title: LINK Spacecraft Set for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: LINK Spacecraft, developed by Katalyst Space, is set to launch on a mission to boost NASA's Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory. The launch is scheduled for no earlier than July 2 from Kwajalein Atoll.

title: NASA’s Webb Studies How Planet Survived Death of its Star
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope is providing new insights into how planets survive the death of their stars. The telescope is studying a Sun-like star that expanded into a red giant billions of years ago.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4934-4940: In the Land of the Polygons
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is exploring a light-toned, smooth unit on Mars, as described in a recent blog post by a senior research scientist. The rover's activities are planned over two sols with detailed observations of polygonal terrain.

title: Caltech Welcomes Astrophysicist Ray Jayawardhana as New President
summary: Caltech has appointed astrophysicist Ray Jayawardhana as its new president, succeeding Thomas Rosenbaum. The appointment was announced on January 6, with Jayawardhana beginning his tenure today.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. The contracts aim to support the development of landers for future lunar missions.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is expediting the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral following a recent New Glenn anomaly that caused significant damage. The company is employing a hybrid plan to restore the facility.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final Atlas satellite launch. Several orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope, marking its first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, providing data to support future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The image highlights Euclid's capability to study the galaxy's dense core.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX completed a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launches include six orbital missions worldwide, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the global launch manifest.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test flight took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This follows a busy month of activities aboard the station.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication showcases milestones from the past five decades.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events aim to inform industry about planned activities and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm is being developed for space applications, aiming to enhance robotic operations in orbit. This technology could revolutionize satellite servicing and maintenance.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Researchers in ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section, including many young contributors, test materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for the agency's missions.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly successfully captured and delivered imagery of a Rocket Lab spacecraft within 61 hours of receiving an order from SSC. The mission was completed ahead of the 72-hour deadline.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space to develop its second military satellite, Aurora, as part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge has revealed plans to construct lunar infrastructure using lunar dust (regolith) to reduce costs for building fission reactors. The approach aims to minimize the need for transporting heavy shielding materials from Earth.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA commended Blue Origin's progress in addressing past mission failures and announced contracts for moon base development. The agency aims for monthly lunar landings using updated lander designs.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions, focusing on lunar landers and their role in upcoming Moon missions. It provides insights into the payloads and objectives of these missions.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic has partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. The collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satcom market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion of Rocket Lab's business beyond launch services.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from Sweden's Esrange Space Center in 2028 using a new franchise model. The collaboration involves SSC Space's facility in northern Sweden.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: ISS Risk Margin ‘Alarming’ As NASA Nears 2030 Extension Date
summary: The ISS faces increasing risks as NASA approaches its 2030 extension deadline, with the chair of a NASA advisory committee warning that operational risk margins have reached an alarming low. Time constraints may jeopardize the station's continued operations.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's LEO constellation has reached a milestone with enough satellites in orbit to support initial service after a ULA launch. The mission, conducted in the early hours of Thursday, enabled continuous service across initial latitudes.

title: Isar Aerospace Lands Big Win With Planet Launch Contract
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a launch contract with Planet to launch one of Planet's next-generation Pelican satellites as early as late 2026. This marks a significant milestone for the German launcher in development.

title: Mitsubishi Electric to Acquire Infostellar
summary: Mitsubishi Electric has announced its intent to acquire Infostellar, a prominent Japanese space sector company. The acquisition aims to strengthen Mitsubishi Electric's position in the space industry.

title: Vodafone Ireland Conducts Emergency Services D2D Call With AST SpaceMobile JV
summary: Vodafone Ireland successfully conducted a test call with AST SpaceMobile's satellite constellation to evaluate connectivity for emergency and first responders. The test is part of efforts to enhance emergency services communication.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the growing importance of satellite networks for non-government applications and the types of apps dominating satellite traffic. It highlights the increasing relevance of satellite internet as thousands of new satellites are launched into orbit.

title: Dish Satellite TV and Wireless Businesses File for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: Dish Network's satellite TV and wireless businesses filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy to facilitate the transition of its Dish Wireless operations after spectrum acquisitions. The move aims to restructure the company's financial obligations.

title: Ovzon Wins New Defense Deal with European NATO Nation
summary: Ovzon secured a $7.1 million defense contract with a European NATO nation for its satellite communications solution. The deal is valued at 74 million Swedish krona.

title: Unseenlabs Orders 2 Cubesats from GomSpace
summary: French radio frequency detection company Unseenlabs has ordered two additional cubesats from smallsat manufacturer GomSpace. The contract is worth 2.2 million euros ($2.5 million).

title: Lite Coms Hires Calspan Exec Greg Campbell as President
summary: Lite Coms has appointed Greg Campbell, a former Calspan executive, as its new president. Campbell will lead the company's daily operations and focus on expanding manufacturing capabilities and investments.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines have secured NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of efforts to establish a lunar base. This initiative supports NASA's broader goals for sustainable lunar exploration.
